rows.notes: Clear centred page year at top of page, no monetary values present; very legible.

rows.description: Domus Fund Payments on acct of
rows.notes: Clear heading immediately below the year; no amounts on this line.
Top-right carry-in total clearly aligned in the three amount columns as 371 pounds, 4 shillings, 8 pence; columns and digits are sharp and validate against currency rules.
Section label 'Subscriptions.' with no monetary amounts in the text area; clear and distinct.
Description is legible, but the small numerals to the right are faint and partly obscured by ruling; unable to unambiguously assign pounds/shillings/pence to this row.
Text 'Medmenham Do' is readable. The amount area is faint and ambiguous; I did not force a numeric reading to avoid column misalignment errors.
Description clear. Amount digits near the ruling are faint and small; multiple plausible readings exist so values are left blank to avoid mis-registration of columns.
Name 'Benson' legible. The amount to its right is indistinct in the image; I could not confidently parse pounds/shillings/pence.
Handwritten place-name is somewhat ambiguous (read as 'Long Wittenam'); amount column is faint here so left blank rather than risk misalignment.
Name (written as 'Gwynear.') legible; numeric columns alongside are small/faded at this height of the scan, preventing confident extraction.
'do Poor of' (appears to be a continuation) is readable; no clear amount visible in the three narrow columns — left blank intentionally.
Text is decently legible; the amount column shows a small mark that might be '10' but it is not crisply aligned – left blank to avoid misread.
Entry text clear; the numeric area contains a small figure that could be 5 but is faint and close to neighbouring rows — ambiguous so left blank.
Description legible. The amount to the right looks like a single digit (5) but is blurred; chosen to leave blank rather than risk wrong column parse.
The right-hand shows '1 1' aligned in the shillings/pounds area; interpreted as 1 pound and 1 shilling. Pence position is blank. Vertical column alignment is reasonably clear.
Handwritten description is moderately legible but the numeric column entries are smudged and look like double digits ('22' & something) — ambiguous; left blank to avoid invalid currency values.
Name readable; the numeric field appears to contain a two-digit figure (24) but its alignment is not certain — left blank for safety.

To the right of 'Corn Prizes' are the digits '9' and '6' in the narrow columns. The exact pence column is blank or unclear; interpreted as 9 pounds 6 shillings (pence not shown). Currency ranges valid.

The line contains additional marginal notes '3.7. & 6.9' but the amount area reads as '10 4' (pounds and shillings). Pence column unclear; chosen pounds=10, shillings=4 based on alignment with ruled columns.
Short pence/shillings area shows a solitary '5' aligned in the pounds column; interpreted as 5 pounds (no shillings/pence visible).

rows.description: Bursar winding up of accounts under the old system
rows.notes: Large, clearly written value in the pounds and shillings columns '595 17' — pence column appears blank. Vertical ruling lines are unambiguous here so extraction is reliable.

rows.description: Gamton Hill wook at Gwynnear Feb. 1868
rows.notes: Numbers '90 11 2' are clearly written and well aligned in the three amount columns (pounds, shillings, pence). All values fall within valid currency ranges.

rows.description: Gwynear Feb.1868 — Im Bill £3; Stamps 6.6; Comm: £10.16.8
rows.notes: This grouped line shows the summed total at the right as '14 3 2' and is clearly aligned beneath the Gwynnear notes. The component bills are transcribed in the description and sum matches the right-hand total.

rows.description: Gwynear Augt — Im Bill 3 17.5; Stamps 6.6; Comm: 12.16.5
rows.notes: The assembled line for 'Gwynear Augt' has a clear right-hand total '17 0 4'. The smaller component figures are written nearby; the right-column subtotal is crisp and validates as currency.
Names 'Bracher' and 'Berry' are legible as a paired entry. The amounts in the narrow columns beside them are partly cut off or faint in the scan; omitted to avoid mis-parsing.
Single name 'James' present beneath the Bracher/Berry lines. Right-hand numeric marks are small and ambiguous; no definite pounds/shillings/pence could be isolated.
Right-hand columns clearly read '7 5 6' adjacent to this entry; digits are well-formed and align with the pounds/shillings/pence columns.
'Turrill Jones' line shows a clear '1 2' in the pounds/shillings area; no pence digit is present or it's blank in the pence column — interpreted as 1 pound, 2 shillings.
The digits '7 8 5' are clearly written to the right of 'Drawing Book'. Alignment with the ruling lines is unambiguous and all currency digits are within valid ranges.
Line reads 'Rose.' then 'Hine Nails'; right-hand shows '1 5' in the pounds/shillings columns. Pence not visible; interpretation as 1 pound 5 shillings is consistent with alignment.
Final bottom-right total 'L. 1235 17 7' is crisply written inside a boxed total area. Column alignment and digits are clear and validate as correct currency values.
